article.description: — LeBron James had 28 points, 12 assists and six rebounds, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers clinched home-court advantage in the first round of the playoffs with their 101-73 victory over the Phoenix Suns on Friday night.
article.headline: LeBron James scores 28, leads Lakers past Suns 101-73 to clinch home-court advantage in 1st round

images.caption: LeBron drops 28 points, dishes 12 assists in the Lakers' win over the Suns.
images.name: LeBron drops 28 points, dishes 12 assists in Lakers' win

article.linkText: LeBron James scores 28, leads Lakers past Suns 101-73 to clinch home-court advantage in 1st round
article.story: LOS ANGELES -- — LeBron James had 28 points, 12 assists and six rebounds, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers clinched home-court advantage in the first round of the playoffs with their 101-73 victory over the Phoenix Suns on Friday night. Luke Kennard scored 19 points and Rui Hachimura added 13 for the Lakers, who will be either the third or fourth seed in the Western Conference despite finishing the regular season without injured stars Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. Los Angeles (52-29) is one game behind third-place Denver (53-28) with one game left, but LA holds the tiebreaker over the Nuggets. The Lakers won their second straight and their 15th in 19 games with a vintage performance from the 41-year-old James, who showed no weariness on the second night of a back-to-back set in which he scored 54 points on 63.6% shooting (21 for 33). James also recorded the 12,000th assist of his career in the first half, becoming only the fourth NBA player to reach the milestone. The NBA scoring king racked up 14 points in the first quarter, and he added six points and seven assists in the second half while making sure the Lakers' lead stayed firm. Dillon Brooks scored 12 points for the Suns, who are seventh in the West standings and locked into the top spot in the play-in tournament. Devin Booker sat out for Phoenix to rest his injured right ankle for the postseason, and Jalen Green also sat with right knee soreness. Collin Gillespie and Grayson Allen played in the first half, but sat in the second half of the Suns' lowest-scoring performance of the season. The Lakers' injury woes remain dire, but Marcus Smart had six points and seven assists against Phoenix in his return from a nine-game injury absence. James scored or assisted on nine of the Lakers' first 10 field goals while they streaked to an early 16-point lead. Up next Suns: At Oklahoma City on Sunday. Lakers: Host Utah on Sunday. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/NBA

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
